bead.title: ddx work: route-preflight timeout is retryable, not a worker-halt/park (D1c)
bead.description:
PROBLEM
Route-preflight timeout is treated as a terminal failure on two paths instead of a retryable reroute: (a) the STARTUP RoutePreflight halts the whole worker via setExit("OperatorAttention") when route resolution is slow-but-fine; (b) the per-bead routeResolutionTimeoutReport parks one bead and records a wedge. A slow-but-successful resolution should reroute/back-off, not halt or park. Re-pin lines against HEAD (search RoutePreflight, routeResolutionTimeoutReport, FailureModeRouteResolutionTimeout in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go).

ROOT CAUSE
Both paths cannot distinguish a real no-viable (which fizeau signals via error) from a slow resolution that would have succeeded. With F1 (fizeau quota fail-open, v0.14.41) the empty-route cause is largely gone; remaining slow resolutions should be retryable.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Startup preflight timeout: do NOT setExit/halt the worker; log + back off + continue (the next claim re-resolves).
2. Per-bead route-resolution timeout: reclassify as a retryable reroute (release lease, no wedge increment), not a terminal operator-attention park. (Coordinate with D3 which gates the wedge counter at this site.)

NON-SCOPE: do not change RESOLVING-phase semantics for a genuine no-viable error (fizeau returns an error there).

ACCEPTANCE
1. New test: a slow-but-successful startup route resolution does NOT call setExit("OperatorAttention") / halt the worker.
2. New test: a per-bead route-resolution timeout releases the lease and leaves the bead retryable (not parked, no wedge increment).
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
